notes:
MergeMole 1.1.1

- Compact cards: a new density option for the PR list. Compact shows everything the full card does, just tighter: smaller type, trimmed spacing, no avatars. Switch anytime in Settings → General → Cards.
- Refresh every minute: the fastest auto-refresh interval is now 1 minute, down from 5, for the folks who live in their review queue. The default stays every 15 minutes.
- When on-device AI can't run, the reason now shows inside the Apple Intelligence provider card itself instead of floating below it.

MergeMole 1.1.0

- Custom tabs: turn any GitHub search into its own tab. One repo, a label, a teammate's PRs, anything search can express. Click New Tab in Settings, build the query with one-click tips, and a live plain-English preview shows exactly what the tab will match. Custom tabs work just like the built-in ones: reorder them, hide them, count them in the menu bar, and AI triages them too.
- A new app icon with the macOS Tahoe glass look. It also now shows up properly rounded on macOS 15, in the installer and in Applications.
- Check for Updates is now in the panel's menu, next to Preferences.

MergeMole 1.0.2

- Now runs on macOS 15 (Sequoia) and later. On-device AI still needs macOS 26 (Tahoe); on older versions you can bring your own AI provider or run without AI.
- Clearer status in Settings when on-device AI isn't available, and the setup buttons now open the right settings tab.

MergeMole 1.0.1

- Now runs on every Mac with macOS 26 (Tahoe), not just the newest release
- A cleaner, smaller installer window

MergeMole 1.0 is here.

MergeMole lives in your menu bar and shows the GitHub pull requests that actually need you, with AI doing the triage so you always know what to look at first.

In this first release:

- On-device AI reads each open PR and ranks it by priority and effort, so the ones that matter rise to the top
- Every PR gets a plain one line summary of what it does, alongside its review state, checks, and merge status
- Everything runs locally on your Mac. It's completely free, and your code never leaves your device
- Want a different model? Connect your own OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, or local Ollama endpoint. Prefer no AI? Turn it off and use MergeMole as a fast, clean pull request list
